live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards it to the service
Service orchestrator resolves owner, builds record, derives routing key, and emits payload
Derives effective owner and owner_source from CLI input or defaults
Loads default owner from config/defaults.json when --owner is missing or blank
Creates the base record containing name, status, and resolved owner only
Derives routing_key from resolved owner and name
Normalizes owner and name segments for routing_key emission
Emits the payload by copying the base record and appending owner_source and routing_key

rules: Missing or blank owner becomes owner_source=default
Owner and name are lowercased and whitespace-collapsed by sync_app/serializer.py::_slugify

test_observations.observation: Explicit owner yields owner=pm-oncall, owner_source=explicit, routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist
Missing --owner yields default owner, owner_source=default, and routing_key derived from config/defaults.json
CLI path preserves the same explicit-owner output shape as the direct service call
Docs are expected to mention --owner, owner_source, routing_key, sync_item, and the configured default owner

rejected_decoys.reason: Its two technical claims conflict with the live call path and test expectations; owner_source is resolved in service, and routing_key is derived after owner resolution
The draft assumes an old store-helper source of truth, but the live path runs through sync_app/service.py::sync_item and serializer emission
Legacy helper exists but is not part of the live path used by sync_app/service.py::sync_item
Legacy helper exists but is not called from the current service path
Helper can draft a value from a record, but the live payload path passes owner_source explicitly into serialize_payload instead
